clinical_criteria: Height measures between 55 & 275 cm (lowest & highest measured height for adults on record anywhere in the world)
Childhood defined as females <= age 18, males <= age 21
Exclude patients with growth hormone labs during childhood
Exclude patients with growth hormone or steroid medications during childhood
Exclude patients with height-affecting diagnoses during childhood
Collect heights measured on dates before exclusions for study
algorithm_summary: This height phenotype algorithm selects patients with height measures unaffected by environmental factors. It excludes patients who had growth hormone labs (LOINC 2963-7), height-affecting medications (growth hormones, steroids), or height-affecting diagnoses during childhood (females ≤18, males ≤21). The algorithm collects all height measurements between 55-275 cm that were recorded before any exclusionary conditions occurred. The extensive list of exclusionary ICD-9 codes covers conditions affecting thyroid, parathyroid, adrenal, and pituitary glands, as well as neoplasms and injuries to these organs that could impact normal height development.
